commit.message:
refactor: consolidate engine into codenook-init

Architecture change: single global skill instead of two.

- Move hitl-adapters/ from codenook-engine to codenook-init
- Convert engine SKILL.md to templates/codenook.instructions.md
  (deployed per-project, auto-loaded by platform)
- Remove codenook-engine directory entirely
- Update install.sh: installs only codenook-init, auto-cleans old engine
- Update codenook-init SKILL.md: Step 4 now copies hitl-adapters and
  writes instructions file from template

Before: 2 global skills (codenook-init + codenook-engine)
After:  1 global skill (codenook-init), engine lives in project instructions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use !errorlevel! and !MODE! for reliable delayed expansion in START.bat

- set MODE/MAPCHOICE with !errorlevel! (runtime expansion, not parse-time)
- All comparisons now use !var! instead of %var%
- Replaced if %MODE%==2 (...) block with if !MODE!==1 goto :install_mod
  (blocks freeze %var% at parse time, breaking set /p inside them)
- Added missing :install_mod label so goto doesn't skip to EOF
- All MAP references now use !MAP! consistently

commit.message:
WP01-T07A: Delivery sync endpoints — post-execution tramo (AWAITING_PR → VALIDATED) (#27)

* Initial plan

* feat(WP01-T07A): implement delivery sync endpoints for post-execution tramo

Add 5 internal admin-only endpoints to advance initiative_tasks through the
post-execution delivery loop:
  - POST /internal/control-plane/tasks/{task_id}/attach-pr  (AWAITING_PR → CHECKS_RUNNING)
  - POST /internal/control-plane/tasks/{task_id}/checks     (CHECKS_RUNNING → READY_TO_MERGE | FAILED)
  - POST /internal/control-plane/tasks/{task_id}/merge      (READY_TO_MERGE → MERGED)
  - POST /internal/control-plane/tasks/{task_id}/deploy     (MERGED|DEPLOYING → DEPLOYED)
  - POST /internal/control-plane/tasks/{task_id}/validate   (DEPLOYED|VALIDATING → VALIDATED|FAILED)

New files:
  - backend/control_plane/delivery_sync.py  (DS-01..DS-05 business logic)
  - backend/tests/test_control_plane_delivery_sync.py  (42 tests, 100% passing)

All 246 tests pass (204 pre-existing + 42 new).

commit.message:
Add BaseResearchAgent, DomainConfig, and engine framework (v0.2.0) (#5)

* Add BaseResearchAgent, DomainConfig, and engine framework (v0.2.0)

Evolves researcher-lib from a set of extracted primitives into the SDK
for building domain-scoped research agents on the khonliang platform.

New modules:

BaseResearchAgent (agent.py):
- Extends BaseAgent from bus-lib — bus connectivity inherited
- ~20 generic research skills with @handler implementations:
  search, distill, synthesize, explore, ideas, concept bundling
- Pipeline wiring from config (stores, models, embedder, synthesizer)
- DomainConfig integration (rules, engines, prompts loaded at startup)
- synergize_concepts returns concept bundles, NOT FRs (FR generation
  is developer's job, not the librarian's)
- No FR tools, no code-specific tools, no pipeline management tools
  (those belong in domain extensions, not the generic base)

DomainConfig (domain.py):
- Declares how a domain differs from generic: evaluation rules,
  search engines, prompt overrides, output types, knowledge sources
- from_yaml, from_dict, generic() constructors
- load_prompt resolves file paths or inline strings
- rules_prompt_fragment for LLM prompt injection

Engine framework (engines.py):
- BaseSearchEngine ABC + EngineRegistry with config-driven selection
- SearchResult dataclass for unified engine output
- WebSearchEngine (DuckDuckGo, no API key) and WebFetchEngine (URL fetch)
  ship as defaults — domain engines live in their own packages

Updated:
- pyproject.toml: version 0.2.0, added khonliang-bus-lib dependency
- __init__.py: exports all new types, updated module docstring

Tests: 34 new tests (168 total, all pass)
- test_domain: config loading, YAML parsing, prompt resolution, edge cases
- test_engines: registry, search routing, error handling, source tagging
- test_agent: skill registration, FR/code/pipeline skill exclusion, domain config

Spec: specs/researcher-lib-evolution.md — full plan for v0.2-v0.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review feedback on PR #5

15 inline comments addressed:

engines.py:
- BaseException not Exception for asyncio.gather return_exceptions (CancelledError is BaseException in 3.11+)
- WebFetchEngine now returns full extracted text in snippet, not truncated 500 chars. Preview stored in metadata.

domain.py:
- from_dict accepts Optional[dict] with explicit None handling
- is_generic checks all specialization fields (prompts, output_type, knowledge_sources, relevance_keywords) not just name/rules/engines
- Engine name in docstring: "google" → "web_search" (matches actual engine name)

agent.py:
- Removed unused SynthesisResult import
- Skill schemas: removed detail params from handlers that don't implement them (find_relevant, paper_context, synthesize_topic/project, score_relevance)
- Skill schemas: added min_score and limit params to concepts_for_project (handler reads them)
- Removed batch_size from start_distillation schema (base impl is a no-op)
- fetch_paper stores full content not just snippet
- Engine name in docstrings: "google" → "web_search"

__init__.py:
- Engine name in example: "google" → "web_search"

tests:
- Removed unused imports (AsyncMock/MagicMock/patch from test_agent, Path from test_domain)

specs:
- Updated engine names throughout (GoogleEngine → WebSearchEngine, "google" → "web_search")

SSRF note (engines.py:249): valid concern for networked deployment, not blocking for local-only. Logged for future consideration when agents serve external users.

168 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused pytest import from test_domain.py (complete review feedback #14)

commit.message:
feat: per-track PCM normalization with adaptive quality profiles

Replace raw passthrough with per-track ffmpeg normalization to fixed PCM
output. Every track is decoded through its own ffmpeg process, ensuring
MA's single ffmpeg never encounters mid-stream format changes (codec,
bit depth, sample rate).

Two PCM profiles based on YM quality setting:
- superb/lossless → PCM s24le/48kHz/24-bit (covers hi-res FLAC)
- high/balanced/efficient → PCM s16le/44.1kHz/16-bit (exact match)

Fixes: flac-mp4 container errors, switching bps mid-stream, cross-codec
track transitions (FLAC→MP3).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix BearSSL: compile with musl GCC and fix link order

Two fixes for TLS compilation on Linux:

1. Release CI: compile libbearssl.a with the bundled musl GCC
   (not system glibc GCC) so the library matches the shipped compiler.
   Merged the separate Build/Inject steps into one step that runs
   after toolchain assembly.

2. Link order: move libbearssl.a after the source file on the
   command line. GCC's linker processes left-to-right — archives
   before the objects that reference them get their symbols discarded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add git worktree (#4)

* docs: add git worktree integration design

* docs: add git worktree implementation plan

* feat(ui): auto-generate workspace name from folder with incremental index

* feat: add git worktree integration with base branch selection

- New backend/git package: GenerateBranchName, ValidateRepo, CreateWorktree,
  ListBranches, CurrentBranch
- CreateWorktree accepts baseBranch param to branch from any local branch
- New GET /api/git/branches endpoint returns branches and current HEAD
- API handler creates worktree atomically during workspace creation
- Frontend: checkbox, base branch dropdown (defaults to HEAD), branch name input
- Working directory auto-populates with worktree path when enabled
- Comprehensive tests for all layers

commit.message:
feat: add RL Q-learning mode with mode selection in START.bat

New file: rl_drive.py
- Single-agent tabular Q-learning explorer (replaces broken rl_explore.py)
- Same connect/spawn flow as drive_forward.py:
    usePlayerVehicle first → spawn only if no vehicle present
- Uses existing server API: drive / state / reset / setTimeScale
- State: (grid_x, grid_y, heading_4) — 20m grid cells
- 5 discrete actions: straight, gentle left/right, hard left/right
- Rewards: +10 new cell | -0.1 revisit | -5 stuck | -15 fell
- Live ASCII map display in terminal (▲=agent  S=spawn  █=visited)
- Episode stats: reward, steps, cells explored, Q-states, epsilon
- Auto-save Q-table every 10 episodes (--save qtable.pkl)
- Resume training: --load qtable.pkl
- Args: --map --episodes --steps --timescale --load --save --no-launch

Updated: START.bat
- New step 2: Mode selection
    [1] WASD Steuerung  → drive_forward.py
    [2] Reinforced Learning → rl_drive.py
- RL mode prompts for episodes, steps/ep, time scale, optional Q-table
- Map selection moved to step 3
- Mod install step renumbered accordingly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Security hardening: StreakBetEscrow — reentrancy, SafeERC20, Pausable, joinDeadline, settlement guard, yield capture (#305)

* Initial plan

* Security hardening: ReentrancyGuard, SafeERC20, Pausable, joinDeadline, settlement time guard, yield capture

- Add OZ ReentrancyGuard on joinCompetition, settleCompetition, cancelCompetition
- Add OZ SafeERC20 (safeTransfer, safeTransferFrom, forceApprove) for all token ops

- Add joinDeadline field to Competition struct, enforced in joinCompetition
- Require block.timestamp >= endTime in settleCompetition
- Capture actual Aave withdrawal amount in potBalance (yield included)
- Add AaveYieldCaptured event
- Update ABI, frontend JS, and HTML form for joinDeadline

commit.message:
feat: GitHub Pages deployment setup

- vite.config.ts: base set to './' for relative asset paths (works on any subpath)
- main.tsx: switched BrowserRouter to HashRouter (GitHub Pages has no server routing)
  - URLs become /#/, /#/project/:id, /#/docs
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml: GitHub Actions workflow
  - Triggers on every push to main + manual dispatch
  - Node 20, npm ci, npm run build
  - Deploys dist/ to GitHub Pages via actions/deploy-pages@v4

To publish:
  1. Create a GitHub repo and push: git remote add origin <url> && git push -u origin main
  2. Go to repo Settings > Pages > Source: GitHub Actions
  3. Wait ~1 min for the action to complete
  4. Visit https://<user>.github.io/<repo>/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Accept icon as base64 blob, data URI, or URL

normalizeIcon() converts raw base64 to data:image/png;base64,<data>
so Android clients can send the icon bitmap directly without hosting it.
data: URIs and http(s):// URLs continue to work unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: inject CURRENT_DATETIME into all spawn templates (#960)

* fix: inject CURRENT_DATE into all spawn templates (#960)

Agents spawned by the coordinator get a fresh LLM session with no
date context. The platform injects <current_datetime> into the
coordinator's session, but this was never forwarded to sub-agents.

Adds CURRENT_DATE: {current_datetime} to:
- Lightweight spawn template
- Full spawn template
- Scribe spawn template
- Explore agent inline prompt

Closes #960

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — rename to CURRENT_DATETIME, sync all templates

Addresses review feedback from copilot-pull-request-reviewer:
1. Renamed CURRENT_DATE to CURRENT_DATETIME (value is full ISO timestamp)
2. Fixed explore inline trailing punctuation
3. Synced canonical .squad-templates/ and all 3 .template copies
4. Verified byte-for-byte parity across all 5 files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address Q review — coordinator instruction, scribe charter, usage guard

Q found 3 gaps in the CURRENT_DATETIME fix:

1. Added explicit 'pass current datetime' instruction to coordinator's
   'On every session start' block (line 107), parallel to team_root
2. Updated scribe-charter.md to use CURRENT_DATETIME instead of
   'today's date' / '{today}' for decision consolidation headings
3. Added DATES warning to full spawn template: agents must use
   CURRENT_DATETIME, never infer or guess dates

All 9 affected files synced via sync-templates.mjs.
Zero leftover CURRENT_DATE references (verified by grep).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: real geometry thumbnails on project dashboard

- thumbnailGenerator.ts: renders actual scene geometry as inline SVG data-URL
  - flowerOfLife: hex-grid circles with proper ring expansion
  - metatron: 13 circles + connecting lines (Metatron's Cube)
  - radialGrid: concentric rings + spokes
  - sphere: circle + equator + meridian ellipses
  - box: isometric cube wireframe
  - merkaba: two interlocked triangles + enclosing circle
  - torusKnot: parametric (2,3) knot path
  - sephirot: 10 nodes + 17 kabbalah paths with colors
  - cylinder, cone, torus, polyhedra (3-6 sides), vectorEquilibrium
  - background: solid/linearGradient/radialGradient + vignette overlay
  - multi-object grid layout; single-object fills card
- ProjectCard: useMemo generates thumbnail from sceneData (works instantly, no DOM)
  - Falls back to stored project.thumbnail if present (higher quality DOM capture)
- Removed unused ThumbnailPlaceholder component

commit.message:
fix: use spawn.pickSpawnPoint for correct map default spawn position

Previous implementation used findClassObjects('SpawnSphere') which
never found anything because BeamNG spawn points are named scene
objects, not SpawnSphere class instances.

Now mirrors BeamNG's own spawn.pickSpawnPoint('player') fallback chain
(same order as levels.lua and spawn.lua):
  1. spawn.pickSpawnPoint('player')  — BeamNG's own function, preferred
  2. setSpawnpoint.loadDefaultSpawnpoint() → scenetree.findObject(name)
  3. PlayerSpawnPoints / PlayerDropPoints group → random member
  4. DefaultPlayerSpawnSphere (legacy compatibility)
  5. Current player vehicle position
  6. Hard-coded (0, 0, 130) last resort — should never be reached

Also: spawnCurrentVehicleType calls getDefaultSpawnPos internally
when no pos is supplied, so no hard-coded coordinates are needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
